blocks.text: The **circulatory system** transports blood throughout the body — carrying **oxygen** and **nutrients** to the tissues and picking up **metabolic waste** for disposal.

blocks.definition: carries blood between the **heart** and the **lungs** for gas exchange.
carries blood between the heart and the rest of the body's tissues.
supports and **lubricates** the heart during contraction.
the **middle** layer; the muscular wall that pumps blood.
the **upper** chambers on each side of the heart.
the **lower** chambers that pump blood out of the heart.

blocks.text: The **interatrial septum** divides the two atria, while the **interventricular septum** divides the two ventricles.

blocks.caption: The three discrete vessel types and how they differ.

blocks.title: Key Points
items: **Plasma** is ~55% of blood volume; **formed elements** make up ~45%.
**Hemostasis** is the process that stops bleeding when a vessel is injured.

blocks.title: Tutor Tip
blocks.body: Remember: **A**rteries carry blood **A**way from the heart — both start with A.

blocks.mermaid: graph LR; Heart-->Arteries; Arteries-->Capillaries; Capillaries-->Veins; Veins-->Heart
blocks.caption: Systemic blood flow (diagram rendering arrives in a later phase).

blocks.text: Blood is a circulating connective tissue.
